title: Rebase Onto — Transplant a Branch
description: You branched `feat/dark-mode` off `feat/theming` instead of `main`. The `feat/theming` branch is not going to be merged. You need to transplant `feat/dark-mode` so it branches directly from `main`, leaving behind the `feat/theming` commits.
objective: The branch `feat/dark-mode` must be rebased directly onto `main`. It must contain only the dark mode commits (not the theming commits). The number of commits on `feat/dark-mode` ahead of `main` must equal exactly the number of commits that were originally unique to `feat/dark-mode`.
hints: `git rebase --onto <newbase> <upstream> <branch>` is the precise tool for this.
`<newbase>` is where you want the branch to land (`main`).
`<upstream>` is the branch you want to cut away (`feat/theming`).
